official_functions.role: Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state
Construct, write, and decode the block_event packet body
Define the block position, event type byte, event data byte, and block registry id body shape
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound block_event frame

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns block_event immediately after block_entity_data.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the block_event id to ClientboundBlockEventPacket.

The official block_event body is block position via RegistryFriendlyByteBuf BlockPos, event type via readUnsignedByte/writeByte, event data via readUnsignedByte/writeByte, and block via ByteBufCodecs.registry(Registries.BLOCK).

The fixture uses the official public ClientboundBlockEventPacket(BlockPos, Block, int, int) constructor with built-in Blocks.NOTE_BLOCK and byte-sized event type/data values, so it does not require initialized Level, BlockEntity, or game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded block position, decoded event type/data, decoded block, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte count are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.
This contract does not prove block event semantics, note block behavior, world/chunk state, initialized game state, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer block event semantics, note block behavior, world/chunk state, initialized game state, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
